method: Cross-site word-form recurrence mass S = sum over multi-sign word-TYPES of max(0, n_distinct_sites - 1), tested against a token->site reassignment null that preserves BOTH word-form frequencies AND per-site token totals (sequential hypergeometric contingency sampling, exact marginal-preserving). perm p = frac(null S >= observed S), one-sided, 2000 draws. Computed for libation (Stone vessel) and admin (Tablet+Nodule+Roundel) corpora separately. L2/L3 structural recurrence only; anonymous sign-tuples; no reading/phonetic/meaning.
result: Libation (stone-vessel) cross-site word-form recurrence is NOT enriched beyond the frequency+site-size reassignment null (S_obs=20 vs null_mean=32.94, perm_p=1.0, ratio=0.607; observed below the entire null range [24,43]). The famous recurring libation forms (A-TA-I-*301-WA-JA at 5 sites, SI-RU-TE at 5, I-PI-NA-MA at 5, JA-SA-SA-RA-ME at 4, U-NA-KA-NA-SI at 3) recur cross-site, but that recurrence is fully explained by their frequencies and per-site token totals. Admin corpus likewise NOT enriched (S_obs=23 vs null_mean=97.08, perm_p=1.0, ratio=0.237). PC passed (detect power=1.0, false-positive rate=0.0). Both corpora behave alike: cross-site recurrence is a frequency+site-size artifact, not a genuine cross-site formula. Genre-dependent lexicon sharing is NOT observed at the structural word-form level.
key_findings: Libation (stone-vessel) cross-site word-form recurrence S_obs=20 is BELOW the reassignment-null mean of 32.94 (perm_p=1.0, ratio=0.607); the observed S lies below the entire null range [24,43]. The libation corpus does NOT have a genuine cross-site formula beyond word-form frequency + per-site token totals.

The famous recurring libation forms (A-TA-I-*301-WA-JA at 5 sites, SI-RU-TE at 5, I-PI-NA-MA at 5, JA-SA-SA-RA-ME at 4, U-NA-KA-NA-SI at 3) recur cross-site, but this recurrence is fully explained by their high frequencies and the site-size distribution (Zakros 128 tokens, Palaikastro 36, Iouktas 33, Syme 20).
Admin corpus is likewise NOT enriched (S_obs=23 vs null_mean=97.08, perm_p=1.0, ratio=0.237) — consistent with prior E032/E053 findings that administrative vocabulary is site-local.

Both genres behave alike at the structural word-form level: cross-site recurrence is a frequency+site-size artifact in BOTH corpora. There is NO genre-dependent contrast in lexicon sharing detectable by this metric.
Positive control passed cleanly: detect power=1.0 (planted formula at all sites, median p=0.002), false-positive rate=0.0 over 25 frequency-only draws (all p>0.05). The machinery is informative.

The reassignment null preserves both marginals exactly (validated by self-check over 2000+ draws), so the negative is not a power artifact: 4 libation sites have >=20 tokens, and the null range is tight.
successor_hypotheses: E072: Test whether libation cross-site recurrence becomes enriched under a NULL that preserves only site totals (not form frequencies) — i.e. is the recurrence driven by a restricted form-set (formulaic vocabulary) even if not by cross-site co-occurrence beyond frequency? This separates 'restricted dedicatory lexicon' from 'cross-site formula beyond frequency'.

E073: Apply the same S + reassignment null to a POSITIONAL/sequential formula metric (fixed sign-sequence at a fixed position in the text, e.g. opening word) rather than bag-of-words recurrence — the libation formula may be positional rather than frequency-anomalous.

E074: Test genre-dependent sharing with a DIFFERENT null: permute forms WITHIN site (preserving site vocabularies) vs ACROSS sites — to detect whether the libation corpus uses a shared vocabulary SET across sites even if per-form frequencies match the null.

E075: Restrict the libation analysis to the 4 high-token sites (Zakros, Palaikastro, Iouktas, Syme, each >=20 tokens) and recompute S + null — confirm the negative is not driven by the many 1-2 token sites.

E076: Compare the libation corpus against other religious/dedicatory supports (Metal object, Stone object, Architecture) pooled, to test whether a broader religious genre shows cross-site formulaic recurrence that the stone-vessel subset alone does not.

E077: Test a BIGRAM/adjacency recurrence metric (fixed sign-pair sequences across sites) rather than whole-word-form recurrence — formulaic structure may live in sign-pair collocations rather than full word-forms.
non_circular: Anonymous word-forms (sign tuples, len>=2); genre = physical support field (given, not inferred from text). The token->site reassignment null preserves BOTH each word-form's total count (frequency) AND each site's total token count (site size), breaking only the form<->site association. Therefore any cross-site recurrence in S is beyond frequency + site-size. L2/L3 structural recurrence only: no reading, no phonetic value, no meaning. PC is fully synthetic (stated).
deviations: None. Prereg + plan_hash frozen before global computation. PC synthetic (stated). Machinery self-check passed (marginal preservation + S correctness + detect/false-positive calibration). Used 2000 null draws (>=1000 required).
